data.title: POET Shares Plummet 47% Following Marvell's Order Cancellation
data.description: POET shares experienced a significant decline, dropping 47% to $7.95 on April 27. According to NS3.AI, this downturn followed Marvell's decision to cancel purchase orders that were inherited from Celestial AI. The cancellation notice was sent by Marvell on April 23, after POET's CFO, Thomas Mika, publicly discussed the relationship and shipment outlook, which Marvell considered a breach of confidentiality. Despite the setback, POET has stated that it remains committed to advancing its product development in the AI and optical networking sectors.

data.title: Token Value Accumulation Mechanisms Considered Secondary to Protocol Revenue
data.description: The primary focus for token value lies in the scale of protocol revenue, rather than mechanisms like dividends or token burning. According to PANews, these mechanisms are seen as supplementary, enhancing the overall value but not serving as the core driver. The emphasis remains on the income generated by the protocol itself.

data.title: STOCKS | Wall Street Eyes Asian Tech Stocks Amid AI Boom
data.description: Major financial institutions, including Fidelity International, Nomura, and JPMorgan Chase, have reiterated their bullish stance on Asian stock markets. According to Jin10, these firms highlight the attractive valuations and promising earnings potential of Asian tech stocks compared to the high valuations of U.S. stocks. The ongoing AI boom is seen as a key driver of growth in the region, sparking increased interest from Wall Street investors.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks: AI Giants Experience Afternoon Decline
data.description: Hong Kong's AI sector saw significant declines in the afternoon trading session. According to Jin10, Zhihu (02513.HK) experienced a drop of over 10%, while MINIMAX-W (00100.HK) fell more than 3%. These movements reflect a broader trend in the market as investors react to recent developments in the AI industry.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Prices Decline Below $4,650 per Ounce
data.description: Gold prices have experienced a short-term decline, falling below $4,650 per ounce. According to Jin10, the precious metal has decreased by 0.69% within the day.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Address Sees Massive Return on Token Investment
data.description: According to Foresight News, monitoring by ai_9684xtpa revealed that an address starting with 9Dq1k purchased 10.46 million tokens just 1 minute and 30 seconds after a SCAM token was deployed, spending 1.7 SOL at an average cost of $0.00001352 per token. Over the past 10 hours, the address sold the tokens at an average price of $0.00453. The profit from this transaction increased from $141.5 to $77,500, achieving a return rate of 54,661%.

data.title: A whale withdrew $9.98 million worth of ETH from Kraken.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 4,361 ETH from Kraken in the past 30 minutes, worth approximately $9.98 million. This address currently holds a total of 4,383 ETH, worth approximately $10 million.

data.title: CleanSpark CEO Discusses AI Data Center Transition Costs
data.description:
Bitcoin mining company CleanSpark's CEO, Matt Schultz, highlighted significant cost increases associated with upgrading mining facilities to AI data centers at the 'Bitcoin 2026' conference. According to Foresight News, Schultz noted that the cost per megawatt has surged from approximately $500,000 to between $10 million and $12 million. Additionally, staffing requirements have increased from about one person per 10 megawatts to around eight people.

Schultz warned the industry against focusing solely on short-term stock price boosts from contract announcements, as leading cloud providers impose stringent lease terms. Delays in delivery could result in losing an entire year's contract revenue.

CleanSpark initially utilizes Bitcoin mining to consume local surplus electricity, aiding power companies in monetizing idle capacity. After establishing partnerships, the company transitions to building AI data centers. This strategy enabled CleanSpark to outbid a trillion-dollar tech giant for a 100MW project in Cheyenne, Wyoming.

Schultz emphasized that Bitcoin mining's interruptible nature allows it to fill power gaps during AI data centers' low-demand periods, assisting power grids in managing renewable energy fluctuations. This makes Bitcoin mining an ideal partner for power companies.

data.title: Tokenization Advances from Hong Kong to Global Markets
data.description: Tokenization is gaining momentum from Hong Kong to global markets. According to PANews, MSX is continuing its progress in this area. The development of tokenization is expected to bring significant changes to the financial landscape, offering new opportunities and efficiencies. As the trend expands, it is anticipated to impact various sectors worldwide.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Nomination for Federal Reserve Chair Advances to Senate Vote
data.description:
Last Sunday, Republican Senator Thom Tillis withdrew his opposition to the confirmation process for Kevin Warsh's nomination as Federal Reserve Chair following the conclusion of a Department of Justice investigation into current Chair Jerome Powell. According to Odaily, the Senate Banking Committee voted 13 to 11 in favor of advancing Warsh's nomination to a full Senate vote. The vote is scheduled for April 29 at 10 a.m. Eastern Time.

On the same day, the Federal Open Market Committee will announce its latest interest rate decision. Current Chair Jerome Powell will host what may be his final press conference after leading the Federal Reserve for eight years. Powell's term as Chair ends on May 15, but his term as a board member continues until January 31, 2028. Whether Powell will also leave the Federal Reserve Board is a key focus for the market.

data.title: On April 29, Warsh's nomination for Federal Reserve Chairman will be voted on by the full Senate.
data.description: Last Sunday, Republican Senator Thom Tillis dropped his obstruction of Warsh's nomination as Federal Reserve Chairman after the Justice Department concluded its investigation into Powell. The Senate Banking Committee ultimately voted 13-11 to send Warsh's nomination to the full Senate for a vote. According to the Senate Banking Committee's website, the vote is scheduled for 10:00 AM Eastern Time on April 29. On the same day, the Federal Open Market Committee will also release its latest interest rate decision, and incumbent Chairman Powell will hold his 63rd, and possibly final, press conference in his eight years at the helm of the Federal Reserve. Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chairman will expire on May 15, but his term as a member of the Board of Governors will run until January 31, 2028. Whether Powell will simultaneously step down from the Federal Reserve Board of Governors has become a focus of market attention.

data.title: Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs recorded approximately $50.4 million in net outflows on April 27, following a brief period of net inflows. According to NS3.AI, data from Farside Investors indicated that Fidelity's FETH was at the forefront of these withdrawals, with a substantial outflow of $48.4 million.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Analyzes Kevin Warsh's Impact on Federal Reserve Balance Sheet Policy
data.description: Arthur Hayes has expressed interest in Kevin Warsh's approach to the Federal Reserve's balance sheet, emphasizing its significance over short-term interest rates. According to NS3.AI, Hayes believes that balance sheet policy plays a crucial role in U.S. dollar liquidity and Bitcoin pricing. He suggests that markets might be misinterpreting Warsh's stance as hawkish and anticipates that the Federal Reserve will manage liquidity through asset swaps while maintaining the capacity for U.S. Treasury debt issuance.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Iran Conflict Alters Gold Dynamics, But Buying Reasons Accumulate
data.description: Following the outbreak of conflict in Iran, Brent crude oil surged by 49%, while gold prices fell by 11%. According to Jin10, several institutions have noted that high oil prices are suppressing global growth, and the pressure on the U.S. fiscal deficit continues to increase. These factors have not weakened the long-term rationale for gold; instead, they have strengthened it.

data.title: Binance to Adjust Maker Fee for COP and PEN Fiat Zones
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform will implement a change in the maker fee for transactions within the Colombian Peso (COP) and Peruvian Sol (PEN) fiat zones, effective from 2026-04-24. The maker fee will be adjusted to 0.25%, a move aimed at enhancing market liquidity while ensuring the sustainability of the trading platform.

The security deposit requirement for merchants operating in the COP and PEN markets will remain unchanged. Additionally, the taker and maker fee rates for all other markets within the P2P Zone will not be affected by this adjustment. Binance emphasizes that transactions conducted in the P2P Block Trading Zone can benefit from up to a 50% discount on trading fees. Furthermore, merchants holding Bronze, Silver, and Gold badges are eligible for discounts of 20%, 30%, and 50% respectively in the Normal P2P Zone.

data.title: Manus Investors Receive Returns Despite Canceled Acquisition
data.description: According to Odaily, insiders have revealed that despite the cancellation of the Manus acquisition, investors in Manus, including venture capital firm Benchmark, have received their returns. Previously, the Office of Foreign Investment Security Review Mechanism (National Development and Reform Commission) made a decision to prohibit foreign investment in the Manus project, requiring the parties involved to cancel the acquisition transaction.

data.title: Although the acquisition was cancelled, Manus investors have already received their returns.
data.description: According to sources familiar with the matter, despite the cancellation of the Manus acquisition, Manus investors, including venture capital firm Benchmark, have already received their returns. Previously, the WSJ reported that the Office of the Foreign Investment Security Review Mechanism (National Development and Reform Commission) issued a decision prohibiting foreign investment in the Manus project, requiring the parties involved to withdraw the acquisition.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $50.4794 million yesterday, with only BlackRock ETHB experiencing a net inflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $50.4794 million yesterday (April 27th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $11.7602 million. ETHB's historical total net inflow has reached $44.0122 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $48.4315 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.291 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $13.532 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.89%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12.048 billion.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: More focused on Warsh's balance sheet stance than on the short-term interest rate path
data.description: At the Bitcoin 2026 conference, Arthur Hayes stated that he is more concerned about the statements made by Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh regarding the balance sheet than the short-term interest rate trend, as this will affect dollar liquidity and the pricing of risky assets such as Bitcoin. Hayes believes that the market's expectation of Warsh being "hawkish" may be over-interpreted, and he anticipates that the Federal Reserve is more likely to adjust liquidity through asset swaps and other means, rather than weakening the US Treasury's debt issuance capacity.
